description:
You are a mobile build engineer who has just received two raw CI logs that
    were automatically copied into your workspace.  
    They live in the directory

        /home/user/build_logs/

    and are named

        build_android.log
        build_ios.log

    1. Read the two logs and extract the following information for each platform:  
       • build result (“SUCCESS” or “FAILURE”)  
       • total number of tests that were executed  
       • number of failing tests  
       • total wall-clock duration converted to pure seconds (an integer)

       Hints on the log structure (do NOT hard-code the values):  
       • Each item is announced on its own line in the form  
         “[INFO] Build status: …”,  
         “[INFO] Total tests run: …”,  
         “[INFO] Tests failed: …”,  
         “[INFO] Total time: … minutes … seconds”.  
       • The files also contain unrelated warnings, multi-line stack-traces and
         timestamps; you need to filter only the lines that matter.  
       • The “Total time” field must be converted from the mixed
         “X minutes Y seconds” format to an integer that represents the total
         number of seconds, e.g. “7 minutes 35 seconds” → 455.

    2. Create the directory

        /home/user/build_reports/

       if it does not already exist.

    3. Inside that directory create a single **tab-separated** summary file
       called

        /home/user/build_reports/build_summary.tsv

       The file **must contain exactly three lines**:

       • Line 1 is the header:  
         platform<TAB>status<TAB>tests_run<TAB>tests_failed<TAB>duration_seconds

       • Line 2 holds the data for the Android build.  
       • Line 3 holds the data for the iOS build.

       Rules for the TSV:  
       • Use a literal tab character (Unicode 0x09) between fields, not spaces.  
       • No leading or trailing whitespace on any line.  
       • End the file with a single newline character.  
       • The order of the columns must match the header exactly.  
       • Numbers must be plain decimals with no thousands separators.

    4. Print the content of build_summary.tsv to STDOUT so that a human
       reviewer (and the automated grader) can immediately see the result.

    5. Do **all** of the above exclusively with POSIX-compatible shell tools
       (grep, awk, sed, cut, sort, tr, paste, etc.).  Feel free to chain
       commands with pipes and run several in parallel using subshells, but do
       not rely on any third-party language runtimes.

    The automated tests will only look for the exact file

        /home/user/build_reports/build_summary.tsv

    and will verify both its presence and its precise content/format as
    specified above.
truth:
Initial files and their full literal contents:

    Path: /home/user/build_logs/build_android.log
    --------------------------------------------------
    [2024-05-22T14:01:07Z] Starting Android build job
    [INFO] Build status: SUCCESS
    [WARN] Deprecated API warnings found: 5
    [INFO] Total tests run: 120
    [DEBUG] Some verbose gradle output…
    [INFO] Tests failed: 3
    [INFO] Total time: 7 minutes 35 seconds
    [2024-05-22T14:08:45Z] Build finished
    --------------------------------------------------

    Path: /home/user/build_logs/build_ios.log
    --------------------------------------------------
    2024-05-22 15:10:03.912 xcodebuild[4231:778921] note: Using new build system
    [INFO] Build status: FAILURE
    [INFO] Total tests run: 110
    [ERROR] Code signing step failed on app-extension target
    [INFO] Tests failed: 11
    [INFO] Total time: 8 minutes 22 seconds
    [INFO] Some post-processing step failed
    --------------------------------------------------

    Expected output file that the grader will look for:

    Path: /home/user/build_reports/build_summary.tsv
    Expected byte-for-byte content (⇥ represents a literal tab character):

        platform⇥status⇥tests_run⇥tests_failed⇥duration_seconds
        android⇥SUCCESS⇥120⇥3⇥455
        ios⇥FAILURE⇥110⇥11⇥502

    (Each line ends with a single newline character “\n”.)

    Directory /home/user/build_reports/ does not exist at start; it must be
    created by the agent.  Both /home/user/build_logs/ and its files are owned
    by user “user” with default 0644 permissions and are readable by the agent.
